commit_message: python script rewrite for new file format

user_prompt: edit `scripts\thefly_audio_decryptor.py` to support both `.rec` and `.fly` encrypted mp3 files now

edit `scripts\thefly_desktop.py` to handle the encountering of all cases of files, so `wav` and `mp3` and `rec` and `fly` files, but these input files can all live in the same sub folder in the database folder. remember to import stuff from `thefly_audio_decryptor` when needed
agent_reply: In [scripts/thefly_audio_decryptor.py](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_audio_decryptor.py:51), `.fly` now decrypts fixed-size encrypted MP3 chunks straight to `.mp3`, while `.rec` keeps the existing encrypted WAV-to-WAV path. It also exposes shared suffix/output helpers for `.rec`, `.fly`, `.wav`, and `.mp3`.

In [scripts/thefly_desktop.py](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_desktop.py:33), the database now uses a single `audio` subfolder for all audio cases. Device sync and `--transcribe-file` now handle `.wav`, `.mp3`, `.rec`, and `.fly`; encrypted `.rec` becomes `.wav`, encrypted `.fly` becomes `.mp3`, and transcription works against the decoded/plain audio file without PlaybackView-style type branching.

One note: the local Python env is missing `cryptography`, so I could not run an actual encrypted decrypt sample here. I made desktop crypto loading lazy, so `--help` and plain `.wav/.mp3` workflows no longer fail just because that package is absent.

In [scripts/thefly_audio_decryptor.py](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_audio_decryptor.py:51), `.fly` now decrypts fixed-size encrypted MP3 chunks straight to `.mp3`, while `.rec` keeps the existing encrypted WAV-to-WAV path. It also exposes shared suffix/output helpers for `.rec`, `.fly`, `.wav`, and `.mp3`.

In [scripts/thefly_desktop.py](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_desktop.py:33), the database now uses a single `audio` subfolder for all audio cases. Device sync and `--transcribe-file` now handle `.wav`, `.mp3`, `.rec`, and `.fly`; encrypted `.rec` becomes `.wav`, encrypted `.fly` becomes `.mp3`, and transcription works against the decoded/plain audio file without PlaybackView-style type branching.

One note: the local Python env is missing `cryptography`, so I could not run an actual encrypted decrypt sample here. I made desktop crypto loading lazy, so `--help` and plain `.wav/.mp3` workflows no longer fail just because that package is absent.

commit_message: added mp3 support to python scripts

user_prompt: hmmmm I did a test, the decryption works, hell, I can "play" it, but a 9 minute song is decoded as 43 minutes and it sounds like... well... basically nothing in the code cares about the sample rate at all, which is probably the core problem
the encoder script needs to actually transcode it into the 64 kbps CBR 16 Khz sample rate stereo 16 bit signed PCM format first, before encrypting
agent_reply: Fixed the MP3 path in [scripts/audio_file_encryptor.py](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/audio_file_encryptor.py:209). It now runs MP3 inputs through ffmpeg first and emits recorder-compatible MP3 before encryption:

I verified with a generated 44.1 kHz / 192 kbps stereo MP3: after `.mp3 -> .fly -> decrypted .mp3`, `ffprobe` reported `sample_rate=16000`, `channels=2`, `bit_rate=64000`, and the file starts with raw MP3 frame bytes (`FF F3...`), not metadata.
Pre-existing edits in `scripts/thefly_desktop.py` and `src/version.c` are still untouched.

Fixed the MP3 path in [scripts/audio_file_encryptor.py](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/audio_file_encryptor.py:209). It now runs MP3 inputs through ffmpeg first and emits recorder-compatible MP3 before encryption:

I verified with a generated 44.1 kHz / 192 kbps stereo MP3: after `.mp3 -> .fly -> decrypted .mp3`, `ffprobe` reported `sample_rate=16000`, `channels=2`, `bit_rate=64000`, and the file starts with raw MP3 frame bytes (`FF F3...`), not metadata.
Pre-existing edits in `scripts/thefly_desktop.py` and `src/version.c` are still untouched.
